reasoning: The baseline reference value is ~192 riots per 30-day period in the year preceding August 2025. For the resolution to trigger, India would need to record over 1,929 distinct 'Riots' in a single 30-day window. This is fundamentally unprecedented, representing almost an entire year's worth of typical riot activity compressed into just a few weeks. Furthermore, ACLED's definition of 'Riots' explicitly requires violent/destructive acts, separating them from vastly more common 'Protests'. While communal violence has increased in 2024-2025, reaching ~65 violent riots per day for 30 consecutive days would require an absolute nationwide collapse or civil war, which is currently not indicated by any prevailing news or geopolitical reporting. The July 2025 methodology update by ACLED adds broad meta-categories and does not change base incident reporting that might artificially 10x the numbers. As such, I set the probabilities near the permissible floor of 5%, scaling up very slightly for longer horizons due to irreducible time-horizon uncertainty.
